title: Google Launches Gemini 3.5 Flash, Its Strongest AI Agent Model Yet
summary: Google unveiled Gemini 3.5 Flash at Google I/O 2026, a model that outperforms its flagship 3.1 Pro on coding and agentic benchmarks while running 4x faster than competing frontier models.
content: Google launched Gemini 3.5 Flash at its annual I/O developer conference, marking a deliberate shift from AI as a conversational tool to AI as an autonomous agent. The model can independently execute coding pipelines, manage research projects, and in internal tests built an operating system entirely from scratch inside Antigravity, Google's new agent-first development environment. It outperforms Google's own Gemini 3.1 Pro on nearly all benchmarks and is 4x faster than other frontier models — with an optimized variant running 12x faster at the same quality. Gemini 3.5 Flash is now the default model in the Gemini app and AI Mode in Search globally, and powers Gemini Spark, Google's new personal AI agent designed to run 24/7 on behalf of users. Google is also readying Gemini 3.5 Pro, which will function as an orchestrator paired with Flash subagents for the most demanding tasks.

title: Anthropic's Claude Mythos Finds 10,000 Critical Vulnerabilities — Patches Can't Keep Up
summary: Anthropic's restricted Project Glasswing, powered by the unreleased Claude Mythos Preview model, uncovered over 10,000 high-severity software vulnerabilities in one month across critical infrastructure — with only 97 patched so far.
content: Anthropic's Project Glasswing has uncovered more than 10,000 high- or critical-severity vulnerability candidates across some of the most systemically important software in the world in just one month, with 1,094 confirmed high- or critical-severity flaws — yet only 97 have been patched. Claude Mythos Preview, restricted to approximately 50 vetted partner organizations, identified a critical CVSS 9.1 flaw in WolfSSL, a widely deployed embedded TLS library used across IoT devices, automotive systems, and industrial controls. The rate of discovery is orders of magnitude faster than remediation: Oracle has shifted to monthly patch cycles, Microsoft warns its patch count will keep rising, and open-source maintainers have asked Anthropic to slow disclosures because they need more time to design fixes. In one standout case, a Glasswing partner bank used Claude Mythos to detect and block a fraudulent $1.5 million wire transfer in real time. Anthropic warns that models with similar capabilities will soon be widely available, urging organizations to harden their defenses before the asymmetric advantage reverses.

title: Trump Abruptly Cancels AI Safety Executive Order After Tech CEO Backlash
summary: President Trump canceled a planned White House signing ceremony for an executive order that would have given the government voluntary pre-release testing access to frontier AI models, after Elon Musk, Mark Zuckerberg, and industry lobbying helped derail it.
content: President Trump canceled the signing event hours before it was scheduled after learning several top AI CEOs would not attend, with xAI's Elon Musk and Meta's Mark Zuckerberg reportedly urging Trump to call it off. The draft executive order would have created a voluntary 90-day pre-release government testing window for frontier AI models — a proposal the tech industry vigorously opposed, fearing delays to model launches. The administration's push for safety testing was originally triggered by Anthropic flagging cybersecurity risks with its Claude Mythos model, which prompted security-focused agencies to recommend proactive vetting of powerful models. Trump told reporters he 'didn't like certain aspects' of the order and worried it could hamper the US lead over China in AI, saying 'I don't want to do anything that's going to get in the way of that lead.' The White House has not announced when or whether a revised version will be signed.

title: Alibaba's Qwen3.7-Max Runs Autonomously for 35 Hours, Achieves 10x Speedup on Custom Chip
summary: Alibaba's new Qwen3.7-Max model ran fully autonomously for 35 hours to optimize code for an unfamiliar proprietary AI chip, achieving a 10x performance gain and outpacing all competing models in the benchmark.
content: Alibaba's Qwen team released Qwen3.7-Max, a proprietary API-only model purpose-built for long-horizon agentic tasks, marking a significant step toward autonomous AI that can work independently for days. In a headline demonstration, the model was given no documentation, hardware specs, or sample code for an unfamiliar custom chip architecture — and spent 35 hours running 432 kernel tests and 1,158 tool calls, ultimately achieving a 10x speedup over the reference implementation, far outpacing GLM 5.1 (7.3x), Kimi K2.6 (5x), and DeepSeek V4 Pro (3.3x). The model also served as a watchdog during its own training, autonomously running over 10,000 checks across 80 hours to detect reward hacking attempts by the model being trained — writing 13 new detection rules and flagging 1,618 violations. Across standard benchmarks, Qwen3.7-Max scores 80.4 on SWE-Verified, nearly matching Claude Opus 4.6 Max (80.8) and DeepSeek V4 Pro Max (80.6). Alibaba has moved away from open-sourcing its flagship models, with Qwen3.7-Max available exclusively through the Alibaba Cloud API.

title: Andrej Karpathy Joins Anthropic to Use Claude to Train the Next Claude
summary: OpenAI co-founder and former Tesla AI director Andrej Karpathy has joined Anthropic's pre-training team, where he will lead a new group focused on using Claude to accelerate its own pre-training research.
content: Andrej Karpathy, one of the original 11 co-founders of OpenAI and former director of AI at Tesla, announced on May 19 that he has joined Anthropic's pre-training team under Nick Joseph. His specific mission is to build a new team focused on using Claude to accelerate pre-training research — a recursive approach in which the model actively participates in developing its own successor. Karpathy posted on X: 'I think the next few years at the frontier of LLMs will be especially formative. I am very excited to join the team here and get back to R&D.' His arrival follows that of John Schulman (another OpenAI co-founder) in 2024, and continues an accelerating pattern of top talent leaving OpenAI for Anthropic; former chief scientist Ilya Sutskever and former CTO Mira Murati have also departed to found their own labs. The move signals Anthropic's bet that AI-assisted research — not just raw compute scaling — is how frontier labs will sustain their competitive advantage.

title: Google Launches Gemini Omni to Fill the Creative AI Gap Left by OpenAI's Discontinued Sora
summary: Google unveiled Gemini Omni Flash, a new multimodal model that generates and edits video from any combination of text, image, audio, and video inputs, directly targeting the demand left by OpenAI's Sora shutdown.
content: Google launched Gemini Omni, a new AI model capable of creating and editing content from nearly any input type — text, images, audio, and video — positioning it to fill the void after OpenAI discontinued Sora's web and app experiences in April 2026. The first model in the Omni family, Gemini Omni Flash, is rolling out through the Gemini app, Google Flow, YouTube Shorts, and YouTube Create, letting users mix inputs to generate cohesive video outputs grounded in Gemini's real-world knowledge and edit them conversationally while maintaining scene continuity and realistic physics. Google is also introducing Avatars, which allow users to create a digital version of themselves using their own voice and likeness for generated clips. Unlike Sora, which was primarily demonstration-focused, Gemini Omni is designed for everyday creative and commercial workflows, with all content automatically carrying an imperceptible SynthID watermark for AI provenance verification. Gemini Omni Flash is available now to Google AI Plus, Pro, and Ultra subscribers, and is launching at no cost on YouTube Shorts and YouTube Create.
